title: Technical Problem Solving, Coding, and Work-Sample Boundaries · Career Evidence Case: Problem Contract
summary: A timed exercise rewards silent pattern recall while the candidate ignores requirements, changed cases, testing, communication, accessibility, security, tool policy, and task proportionality. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the problem contract increment in observable technical interview practice system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.
Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Problem Contract · Theory Problem Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Problem Contract contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Problem Contract · Theory Problem Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation. For Predict Problem Contract · Theory Problem Contract, keep Problem Contract connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Problem Contract condition decided your prediction.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The worked example variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation. A defensible solution applies Problem Contract, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to observable technical interview practice system.

content.code:
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-3
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Beginning to code immediately demonstrates seniority. This fails the Problem Contract contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: O*NET Software Developers Occupation Report (O*NET current occupation data reviewed 2026-07-15) — Controls task, work-activity, skill, knowledge, work-style, and technology evidence used to decompose roles rather than chase job-title labels.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.
